ps140: 1, Podoben “The joy of the heavenly ranks…”

Let us, joined in faith,* glorify the passion and works of the godly pair.* Let our minds rise like feathers to imitate their zeal,* and let us diligently pray to Christ saying:* "O You who glorify Your saints, as a Good one,* fortify also us by the fear of You!"

O wise ones, you received what was in accordance with your names,* and in a very similar manner* you accomplished the works of God,* having worked for Him in your right paths.* And you truly drank of the salvific chalice of suffering!

You left godlessness as if crossing a sea,* having honorably entered the land of no passions -* the Kingdom of God,* where the stream of nourishment flows,* and the never-changing light shines,* O martyrs of Christ!
theotokion: Extinguish, O Virgin,* the burning desires of my perilous passions* by sprinkling your rain of divine will,* so that I can, like the youths in Babylon,* thank God Who came from you,* while glorifying and blessing your kindness, O Lady!
stavrotheotokion: Seeing the Lamb lifted up upon the Cross,* the immaculate Virgin cried aloud, weeping:* “O my Child most sweet,* what is this new and most glorious sight?* How is it that You Who holds all things in Your hand** was nailed to the Tree in the flesh?”
